metadata.sysdescription: Titan is a USFP industrial anchor where Terran heavy fabrication supports fleet maintenance, station construction, and the endless demand for standardized parts. It’s not glamorous—Titan’s pride is reliability—yet its yards and warehouses quietly influence regional power because whoever controls Titan’s output controls readiness. Civilians live in the shadow of production quotas and dock schedules, and the TSN presence is constant, both as customer and as guarantor of stability.

TPTOR.description: Titan Prime Orbital Traffic Ring. The system’s traffic-control center. Holds the record for issuing the largest fine in the USFP.

Keelwright Certificaion.description: Where ships become legal: inspectors, test rigs, and the dreaded stamp that can ground a vessel for months.

Coil & Crown.description: Ultra-sterile assembly habitat for warp-field coils and precision drive subcomponents

FCS1.description: Auto-surveyed fuel collection zone. Estimated radius: 8,879 units. Local nebular density index: 45.5. Source field: Nebula Field 11. Survey profile: cool deuterium traces threaded through ionized gas; field stability stable; collector saturation estimate 55%. Telemetry confidence: moderate. Cycle filters often; particulate load may spike without warning.

FCS3.description: Auto-surveyed fuel collection zone. Estimated radius: 6,999 units. Local nebular density index: 18.4. Source field: Nebula Arc 3. Survey profile: thin helium wash over denser collection pockets; field stability slowly drifting; collector saturation estimate 92%. Telemetry confidence: limited. Hold station near the inner third for the cleanest skim.
